perturbation.original_text: In the event that the Parties or any of their Representatives are requested pursuant to, or required by, applicable law, regulation, rules of any national stock exchange, or legal process to disclose any of the Information, the Parties must notify each other promptly so that they may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy or, in the sole discretion of the party that initially furnished such Information, waive compliance with the terms of this letter agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the Parties or any of their Representatives are requested pursuant to, or required by, applicable law, regulation, rules of any national stock exchange, or legal process to disclose any of the Information, the Parties may notify each other when reasonably practical so that they may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy or, the party can disclose such information without any prior notice.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'must notify' to 'may notify when reasonably practical' and also, 'the party can disclose such information without any prior notice', it contradicts the original intent of mandatory notification and prior consultation. This creates uncertainty and undermines the original purpose of allowing the other party to seek a protective order and goes against the strict confidentiality and disclosure of information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties and their Representatives (i) will keep the Information strictly confidential and will not (except as required by applicable law, regulation or legal process, and only after compliance with paragraph 2 below), without the prior written consent of the other party, disclose any Information in any manner whatsoever, and (ii) will not use any Information other than in connection with the Transaction; provided, however , that the Parties may reveal the Information to their Representatives (a) who need to know the Information for the purpose of evaluating the Transaction, (b) who are informed by the Parties of the confidential nature of the Information and are provided with a copy of this Agreement, (c) who agree not to disclose any of the Information to any other party and (d) who agree to act in accordance with the terms of this letter agreement to the same extent as if they were parties hereto.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the mandatory 'must notify' to the optional 'may notify when reasonably practical' and adds 'the party can disclose such information without any prior notice.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which requires strict confidentiality and prior written consent before disclosing any information, 'and only after compliance with paragraph 2 below'. The changed text creates an ambiguity and inconsistency that undermines the original confidentiality agreement and the requirement for prior consultation, making it a clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: If COMPANY or COMSYS determines not to proceed with the Transaction, they will promptly inform the other Party of that decision and, in that case, and at any time upon request of COMPANY or COMSYS or any of their Representatives, the other party will either (i) promptly destroy all copies of the written Information in its or its Representatives’ possession and confirm such destruction to the initiating company in writing or (ii) promptly deliver to the initiator of such action all copies of the written Information in its or its Representatives’ possession.
perturbation.changed_text: If COMPANY or COMSYS determines not to proceed with the Transaction, they may or may not inform the other Party of that decision and, in that case, and at any time upon request of COMPANY or COMSYS or any of their Representatives, the other party will either (i) promptly destroy all copies of the written Information in its or its Representatives’ possession and confirm such destruction to the initiating company in writing or (ii) keep with them until being specifically asked to destroy the information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will promptly inform' to 'may or may not inform' introduces an option for the parties to not to disclose whether the transcation will continue or not. In addition, it also changes to, the other party will (ii) keep with them until being specifically asked to destroy the information. The original definition states that when the transaction ends, all parties should destroy the written informaiton in their possession, introducing a contradiction. It undermines the agreed-upon protocol and increases the chance that confidential information is misused after the agreement terminates.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If COMPANY or COMSYS determines not to proceed with the Transaction, they may or may not inform the other Party of that decision and, in that case, and at any time upon request of COMPANY or COMSYS or any of their Representatives, the other party will either (i) promptly destroy all copies of the written Information in its or its Representatives’ possession and confirm such destruction to the initiating company in writing or (ii) keep with them until being specifically asked to destroy the information. Any oral Information will continue to be subject to the terms of this letter agreement. Notwithstanding anything contained in the foregoing to the contrary, either Party may retain in the files of its legal counsel such documents and records as are required to be maintained in order to satisfy any law, rule, or regulation to which the Party is subject or for the purpose of determining its continuing obligations under this Agreement, including any rule of any national stock exchange.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original agreement regarding the destruction of written information upon the termination of a transaction. The original text mandates the prompt destruction of information, while the changed text introduces the option to retain the information until specifically requested to destroy it, directly contradicting the original requirement and creating an inconsistency in the handling of confidential information. This contradicts with the original and the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties hereby acknowledge that, unless and until a definitive agreement between COMPANY and COMSYS with respect to any Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither the COMPANY nor COMSYS will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such Transaction and hereby waive, in advance, any claims (including, without limitation, breach of contract) in connection with any Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties hereby acknowledge that, unless and until a definitive agreement between COMPANY and COMSYS with respect to any Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither the COMPANY nor COMSYS will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such Transaction and hereby waive, in advance, any claims (including, without limitation, breach of contract) in connection with any Transaction, except if the party has already made some sort of investment in anticipation of the said transaction.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'except if the party has already made some sort of investment in anticipation of the said transaction' to Paragraph 5, the party can now sue, contradicting the original part where, the part waive, in advance, any claims. This creates ambiguity regarding the extent of liability and goes against the original agreement, because the party didn't mention how big the investment should be. Therefore any investment will be enough reason to sue.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties hereby acknowledge that, unless and until a definitive agreement between COMPANY and COMSYS with respect to any Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither the COMPANY nor COMSYS will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such Transaction and hereby waive, in advance, any claims (including, without limitation, breach of contract) in connection with any Transaction, except if the party has already made some sort of investment in anticipation of the said transaction. In addition, neither Party will be under any obligation to negotiate a definitive agreement, by virtue of this or any written or oral expression, discussion or negotiation with respect to such Transaction by any or either of the Representatives of the Parties except, in the case of this Agreement, for the matters specifically agreed to herein. 2 6. The Parties hereby agree that money damages may not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that neither party would be under any legal obligation and waived all claims in connection with any transaction. The changed text adds an exception: 'except if the party has already made some sort of investment in anticipation of the said transaction.' This creates a direct contradiction because the parties are no longer waiving all claims, as the changed text introduces a scenario where claims can be made, thus creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: COMPANY agrees that, for a period of eighteen (18) months from the date of this Agreement, unless COMPANY shall have been specifically invited in writing by COMSYS, neither COMPANY nor its Representatives as advisor to COMPANY as principal will in any manner, directly or indirectly: (a) effect or seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect, or cause or participate in or in any way assist any other person to effect or seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect or participate in, (i) any acquisition of any securities (or beneficial ownership thereof) or assets of COMSYS or any of its subsidiaries, (ii) any tender or exchange offer, merger or other business combination involving COMSYS or any of its subsidiaries, (iii) any recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or other extraordinary transaction with respect to COMSYS or any of its subsidiaries, or (iv) any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote any voting securities of the other Party; (b) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (as defined in the Securities Act of 1934) with respect to the securities of COMSYS; (c) otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control or influence management or the board of directors or policies of COMSYS; (d) take any action which might force COMSYS to make a public announcement regarding any of the types of matters set forth in (a) above; or (e) enter into any discussions or arrangements with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing; provided , however , that if COMSYS makes a public announcement that it has entered into a definitive agreement that contemplates a business combination transaction between COMSYS and a third party that would result in a change of control of COMSYS, or in the event any third party has made a tender offer for a majority of the then outstanding securities of COMSYS entitled to be voted generally in the election of directors, then COMPANY may make a proposal to the board of directors of COMSYS with respect to a business combination transaction involving COMPANY and COMSYS.
perturbation.changed_text: COMPANY agrees that, for a period of eighteen (18) months from the date of this Agreement, unless COMPANY shall have been specifically invited in writing by COMSYS, neither COMPANY nor its Representatives as advisor to COMPANY as principal will in any manner, directly or indirectly: (a) effect or seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect, or cause or participate in or in any way assist any other person to effect or seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect or participate in, (i) any acquisition of any securities (or beneficial ownership thereof) or assets of COMSYS or any of its subsidiaries, (ii) any tender or exchange offer, merger or other business combination involving COMSYS or any of its subsidiaries, (iii) any recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or other extraordinary transaction with respect to COMSYS or any of its subsidiaries, or (iv) any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote any voting securities of the other Party; (b) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (as defined in the Securities Act of 1934) with respect to the securities of COMSYS; (c) otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control or influence management or the board of directors or policies of COMSYS; (d) take any action which might force COMSYS to make a public announcement regarding any of the types of matters set forth in (a) above; or (e) enter into any discussions or arrangements with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing; provided , however , that even if COMSYS makes a public announcement that it has entered into a definitive agreement that contemplates a business combination transaction between COMSYS and a third party that would result in a change of control of COMSYS, or in the event any third party has made a tender offer for a majority of the then outstanding securities of COMSYS entitled to be voted generally in the election of directors, then COMPANY will never make a proposal to the board of directors of COMSYS with respect to a business combination transaction involving COMPANY and COMSYS.
perturbation.explanation: By stating 'COMPANY will never make a proposal to the board of directors of COMSYS' introduces a contradiction. It creates uncertainty and undermines any kind of agreement because it removes all the possiblity for COMPANY to give COMSYS any business proposals, against business ethic, and also the purpose of the agreement itself. Which means that all parts of the agreement are useless.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties hereby acknowledge that, unless and until a definitive agreement between COMPANY and COMSYS with respect to any Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither the COMPANY nor COMSYS will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such Transaction and hereby waive, in advance, any claims (including, without limitation, breach of contract) in connection with any Transaction. In addition, neither Party will be under any obligation to negotiate a definitive agreement, by virtue of this or any written or oral expression, discussion or negotiation with respect to such Transaction by any or either of the Representatives of the Parties except, in the case of this Agreement, for the matters specifically agreed to herein. 2 6. The Parties hereby agree that money damages may not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original clause by stating that 'COMPANY will never make a proposal to the board of directors of COMSYS'. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' section, which acknowledges the possibility of a 'Transaction' between COMPANY and COMSYS and discusses the conditions under which neither party will be under obligation with respect to such a transaction. By removing the possibility of COMPANY making a proposal, the changed text undermines the potential for any 'Transaction' discussed in the contradicted text, creating a clear and direct conflict.
